title: Answering Rubbe - Talking about jobs in the new age - request

transcript.text: Hello, I'm here to answer comments on my page, so let's go, let's do, this one is, Rube, R-U-B-B-E, goes five days ago, what do you think about jobs? Do people work way too much? I think four to five hours a day should be enough, maybe I'm lazy.

Um, yeah, four to five hours sounds good. Um, Americans work the most out of everybody, 55 weeks out of the year. Um, I think people do work way too much, but, um, I don't work way too much, so that's not my problem.

Um, personally, if I were to, if I was a doctor, I'd prescribe people to work less and share their shit, you know? Somebody should open up a website, you know, like couch surfing, it's the website where you can stay at people's houses, somebody should open up a website, just exactly like couch surfing, the way that couch surfing is structured, sharing your shit, like your mountain bikes, your kayaks and stuff, that way you don't have to work so hard to buy your stuff.
Um, and, uh, you know, teach people responsibility and sharing and all that stuff, I think it could work.

But, uh, I know in the days of hunter-gatherer, those guys only worked about three hours a day, and then the rest of the day they were picking, popping zits on each other's backs and, you know, massaging each other and stuff, and, um, and I think that's about natural.
I think once we get into the utopia, and we don't need, you know, we'll have free energy and we won't need to work, um, we'll start to work about three or four hours a day.
But even, even the people who are workaholics, even people who have, I mean, everybody I think is going to have a job that they love.
But even those people who have jobs that they love, you know, even, even they love their job, you know, like say you're an artist, oh, I love being an artist.
I don't think they're going to, I, I think even those people aren't going to be working eight hours a day.
I think those people are going to be working, yeah, 45 hours a day, maybe three hours a day, stuff like that.
Um, because, you know, there's other stuff to do.
I think people are going to become more well-rounded and be interested in doing other stuff.
Being a human is about traveling, doing sports, hanging out with your friends.
Uh, we're social beings, doing social things, and, um, four to five hours a day is about right, I think, man.
I mean, that's a lot, that's about how long you can sit still with before your legs start hurting, and that's about how long you can stay focused.
Normally people can only stay focused for about an hour, and then they'd have to take, like, a five-minute break.
Um, so, um, yeah.
Um, I think working a lot, um, causes people to be, uh, stressed out and stuff.
Now, maybe I'm lazy.
Well, if you work that much, you're not lazy.
I think we should re-evaluate the word lazy.
Um, especially in America.
I mean, I've traveled around a lot, and I know in other countries, they're lazy, and they don't have a problem with being lazy.
Um, but that's a whole other subject with different parameters and rules.
But, um, as far as the American concept of lazy, you know, a lot of Americans think they're lazy just if they don't work.
You know, if they hang out and they're doing other stuff.
If, you know, they'd be like, what did I accomplish this week?
What did I accomplish today?

You know, especially in these days when it's really not really that important to look at it that way, because you're going through a lot of, you know, physical changes, mental changes, absorbing a lot.
But, and sometimes, you know, it does you good to just hang out, you know, and just sit there.
You know, zone out and stuff.
Um, but I think the, what we should consider laziness to be is, um, somebody who doesn't do their responsibilities.

Somebody who's like, meet me at 3 o'clock in front of the movie theater to take me there, and you show up at 3.15 because you were fucking, you were out there in front of the computer looking at something.

You didn't have to, you know, that's lazy, or you stayed in bed too long, or laziness is, is not coming through for somebody else, um, or not doing what you said you were going to do just because you couldn't motivate to get out of bed or do that day because you were, you were sucked to do something else the rest of the day.
That's, that's lazy.
Um, but as far as like somebody's own life, whether they're accomplishing this, that, or the next thing, that's not, um, there's no such thing as laziness, you know.
Not, not doing your job, basically, is laziness.
Um, what, so, as far as jobs in general, what do I think about jobs?
Well, yeah, I mean, we're going to, the world is changing a whole lot regarding jobs.
Um, jobs are going to become a lot more, uh, self-motivated.
I've, you know, I've mentioned this a lot in the past, but jobs are going to become a lot more, um, less rigid in far, as far as what, how many, how many years you can, um,
Whatever it's standing by, how many years you can, uh, you're gonna, they're gonna want you to, uh, commit to a job, you know.
Right now it's like, oh, we train you, because people are really selfish that way.
Well, we paid to train you, well, maybe you can pay to train yourself, you know.
You can work in training period for free or whatever.

And then, and then instead of being like, oh, promise us five years, you can be like, yeah, we train you, and promise us six months, so you do six months here, do six months there, do six months here.
That way you can take time off, you can travel around, instead of having to work 55 months out of a year every single year.
And if you, and if you, you know, you want to take a year off, then it's like, you can't come back to get your job, because they've hired somebody else, you know.

Which doesn't make any sense, because if they're always letting people go, or letting people go, not as far as, like, laying off, but letting them go as in letting them do their little adventure, then they're always gonna be needing new people, you know.
It's a circulation thing, we need to speak, we need to think circulationally.
If you, if you pin to, if you pin to up energy, if you pin up energy, you stop energy, it's gonna coagulate and rot and, uh, cause sickness.
If you let the energy flow and grow and flow and grow, then it's gonna grow, you know.
You, you grow muscles by working out, you don't grow muscles by sitting there.
Sitting there is gonna give you tumors, um, and toxin coagulation.
So, it goes the same way as work, you know.
You let people work here and then move on to the next job, and move on to the next job, they're gonna be a lot more cheery, happy.
Vibration's gonna be higher, they're gonna be more motivated, they're gonna be more, uh, productive.
Um, worker productivity is a lot more, um, um, related to motivation and happiness and all that.
Stuff than it is to experience, you know.
I mean, anybody can do a job.
Anybody can do 99% of the jobs after a little bit of work.
Even, like, say, a teacher.
They're like, oh, after three years, man, you're gonna way better teach you than after the first year.
Well, yeah, but what if you're, you know, what if you don't want to be a teacher?

You may be, you may have your, you may have your system down a lot better, but the system that you've got down is the system of, you know, getting by with the least amount of work or, or pissing kids off.
I mean, growing up, I know, growing up, it was the old teachers.
There's, the old teachers who've been at it for a long time who I liked the least because they, because they, well, number one, they were just grumpy.
And number two, they were just, they were just, um, they were doing something that was out of date.
Because, you know, stuff that teaching industry is always changing anyway.
So, basically, that's my example with that.
Um, and, you know, also being self-motivated.
Uh, in the future, pretty soon, we're going to, due to, um, relaxing of, um, uh, governmental regulate, regulations, number one, and also subsidizes, subsidies.
And, you know, the whole subsidy thing is a whole sham anyways because you don't even know what's being subsidized.

You go to the internet, go to the government websites and find out how much money they're, they're subsidizing the, you know, the cow growers and, like, huge, you know, pesticide farms and all this stuff.
You're not going to find it, man.
I've looked.
I've already tried.
You're not going to know.
It's a secret.
It's a secret how much they subsidize stuff.

The only way you're going to know how much they've spent on the Department of, uh, Education or the FCC or whatever, you can go to Wikipedia and, you know, there's $20 billion a year on the FCC or, you know.
And this kind of stuff, $20 billion a year on war on drugs and such, but that's all.
You're not going to know, like, how much they spend on this, this farm and that farm and the next farm.
And just because you don't know, you know, it's, you know, it's all bullshit.
You know, it's, it's, it's criminal, criminal behavior.
Um, so, and because, you know, because of that, you, um, oh my God, where the fuck was I going?
Uh, oh yeah.
Because of that, you know, that, that, uh, you know, the government favors these big corporations and that's why people don't like NAFTA.
I mean, NAFTA is good.
The only problem with NAFTA is that they, they favor the big corporations.
So once, anyways, once we make it transparent, we fix everything, government's not going to have to subsidize, be able to subsidize big corporations and such.
And, um, people can finally start to blast apart and, uh, do whatever they want.
And, uh, companies are going to get a lot smaller.

Um, and, uh, you know, as far as, you know, even as far as like, um, franchises, I think franchises are great, but, um, because they, because, you know, you can become familiar with this guy's breeder, that guy's breeder, whatever.
But, but I think franchises are eventually going to break up any two.
But anyways, my point is, um, people are going to be a lot more self-motivated because it's going to be, people are just going to be, um, you know, we're going to relax in the litigiality of things.
People are going to not sue each other as much.
So people are going to be able to open up their own companies and stuff and be a lot self-motivated, more self-motivated.
And they're going to be able to do their work and have control of their own jobs and stuff.
And, you know, if they do, it's going to be harder for them to take time off.

So therefore, well, no, actually maybe it won't, you know, you can own your own, like, you know, uh, your own construction company and then just take time off and they can call you and you'd be like, Hey, what's up?
I'm on vacation this year.
And you'd be like, okay, but, but I refer you to my friend, Jimmy Joe.
And this is, I refer to him and then Jimmy Joe goes on vacation and he can refer the people who call him to you, you know?
And so anyways, it's going to, um, we're going to be able to get, get by fine on working that just four or five hours a day.
Um, but we're going to still work, you know, people love to do what they do and they love helping people and love, love getting stuff done.
People are always going to want to have more money to do stuff, which is a good thing because you need people to work anyways.
And, you know, make the balance to create stuff.
So, um, you know, and have kids and stuff.
So, uh, people are always going to be motivated to work.

It's just that how much do you want to work and how much you're going to, but you know, just, it's all going to, people are going to, whatever people do and people work, you're going to, I mean, for me, me, my, my passion is making YouTube videos, creating website.

And, uh, well, I thought it was riding my bike since I get my bike back, you know, and that kind of stuff again, eventually turn into a job, you know, and other people, that's just, that's my personality, but other people just like to build, you know?
And so those people who build houses and stuff, they don't do it because they have to, or in the future, they're not going to do it because they have to.
I mean, they're going to do it because they want to, they're, they're going to be out there like hiking a mountain.
It's going to be something they want, they're, they're doing just temporarily, but what they really like to do and enjoy doing is building houses, you know?
And that's, what's going to keep us going.
And if there's any, if there's a job out there that nobody likes to do, like cleaning toilets, just invent a machine that can do it.
We have the intelligence to invent machines that do that.
And then the guy can, the guy who fixes the machine to clean the toilets is going to do it because he likes to, you know?
Because there's plenty of people out there who like to build stuff.
Rubik's cube, you know, that's a toy.
There's plenty of toys that cater to people, little kids who like to build stuff, you know?
So anyways, um, there's not going to be any people out there in the future who do jobs just because they want, just because they have to make money, you know, pretty soon.
So that's basically what I think about work, jobs.
